title: potentilla glandulosa subsp. pseudorupestris
intro: Potentilla glandulosa subsp. pseudorupestris, also known as the glandular cinquefoil or false cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Potentilla glandulosa subsp. pseudorupestris have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. This plant can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
